title: rubus luticola
intro: Rubus luticola is a lesser-known species of the Rubus genus, commonly referred to as the marsh dewberry or lowland dewberry, a type of bramble native to eastern North America. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Rubus luticola include potential uses in treating wounds, fever, and digestive issues, as well as its ability to support cardiovascular health and boost the immune system. Herbal preparations of Rubus luticola can be made into teas, infusions, and salves, which can be used topically or internally, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
